rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of referenced identifiers.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE contracts in valid scopes matching component type or implementation structures.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope to avoid unresolved reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless they are valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
